title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 71.
content:
There was an Old Man in a pew,
    Whose waistcoat was spotted with blue;
    But he tore it in pieces,
    To give to his nieces, - 
    That cheerful Old Man in a pew.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 71.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
